description_long: This is a weathered log cabin with a rectangular footprint, constructed from horizontally stacked logs and featuring a prominent stone chimney on one side. The roof is pitched and covered with wooden shingles, but it is heavily damaged, with a large central hole and numerous missing or broken shingles. One side of the cabin displays a single window with a simple frame, while the adjacent wall has a wide doorway with no visible door attached. The log walls are reinforced with diagonal wooden braces on the longer sides, adding structural stability and a rustic appearance. The overall condition suggests abandonment or significant disrepair, with exposed interior space visible through the roof and open doorway. The cabin's exterior is dominated by natural wood tones and rough stone, giving it a distinctly rustic and utilitarian character.
description: A dilapidated log cabin with a damaged shingle roof, stone chimney, open doorway, and a single window, showing signs of abandonment and structural wear.
description_view_0: The cabin is seen from the end with the stone chimney and intact log wall, with the damaged roof and missing shingles visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the long log wall with diagonal wooden braces and the stone chimney at the far end, as well as the large hole in the roof.
description_view_2: The opposite end reveals a window and a wide open doorway, with the damaged roof and exposed interior clearly visible.
description_view_3: The other long side shows more diagonal braces, the stone chimney, and the extensive roof damage, with the interior exposed through the hole.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with significant shingle loss and a large central hole, constructed from wooden shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: one medium-sized rectangular window, single-pane, asymmetrically placed on the end wall near the doorway
attribute_door_placement: one wide doorway located on the end wall, offset to the right of the window
